topic: A person accidentally sends a text to the wrong recipient
raw_output:
[story 1]

My thumb hovered over the screen, a clumsy, aimless gesture. I was trying to send a quick, silly meme to Sarah, but my finger slipped, landing squarely on a contact labeled 'Mr. Henderson.' A cold knot tightened in my stomach. I stared at the message, a string of embarrassing emojis blinking mockingly. Panic began to bubble up, a hot, prickly sensation behind my eyes. I frantically swiped to find the delete button, my movements jerky and uncoordinated. The silence of my small apartment suddenly felt enormous, amplifying the frantic thumping in my chest. I held my breath, waiting for the notification that would confirm my monumental error.

[story 2]

The phone buzzed, and my heart leaped into my throat. It was a notification, but the content sent a sickening lurch through me. I reread the message three times, my breath catching in shallow gasps. It was meant for my boss, a casual joke about the quarterly report, but the recipient field was completely wrong. My hands started to shake, the smooth glass of the phone slick beneath my grip. I paced the length of my living room, the floorboards seeming to amplify the rapid, shallow breaths I was taking. Every muscle in my jaw felt tight, and I kept glancing toward the door, half-expecting someone to walk in and witness my unraveling.

[story 3]

I felt a wave of nausea wash over me as I looked at the screen. The words I’d typed—a completely inappropriate inside joke—were now visible to the wrong person. A tremor ran through my hands, making it hard to steady the device. I slumped onto the sofa, burying my face in my arms, trying to muffle the ragged sounds escaping my throat. The world narrowed down to the harsh glare of the screen and the overwhelming sense of having irrevocably messed up something important. I squeezed my eyes shut, willing the feeling to subside, but the tension remained coiled tight in my shoulders, refusing to release.
